mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the operational overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the gcloud command-line tool to manage GKE resources.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> This command installs the Google Cloud SDK.
> Refresh the shell to access gcloud commands.
> Initialize the gcloud environment and authenticate.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates kubeconfig to point to your GKE cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

```

> Enables cluster autoscaling.
> Enables monitoring with Stackdriver.
commands: gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Use --quiet to skip confirmation prompts.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to identify and delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes.

```bash

gcloud compute addresses list

```

> Check for any static IPs that may need to be deleted.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your workloads, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.
commands: gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and consider scaling the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Google Cloud account has the required IAM roles for GKE.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the pod is failing to start due to an application error.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE features such as Autopilot mode and improved workload identity.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy cluster autoscaler; use the new version instead.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that all workloads are compatible with the new features.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and authenticated.
